name: Fried Fortune Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut white paper into strips, approximately 3-inches long and 1/2-inch wide. Write a unique fortune on each strip (e.g., "You will have a happy day." or "You will make a new friend soon").
2. Heat the griddle over medium heat (350°F to 375°F for electric skillet). Generously grease the hot griddle using a pastry brush.
3. In a bowl, beat the egg with a fork until foamy. Beat in the sugar until light and fluffy. Stir in the all-purpose flour, salad oil, water, vanilla, and cinnamon until smooth.
4. Drop spoonfuls of batter onto the greased griddle to form circles about 3-1/2 inches across. Use the back of a spoon to spread the batter into thin circles (about 1/4 inch thick). The cookies must be very thin.
5. Cook for about 3 minutes, or until light brown and crisp on the edges. Check with an edge and peek.
6. Turn the cookies using a pancake turner and cook about 3 more minutes on the other side.
7. Lift each cookie onto a plate. Place a paper fortune in the center of each cookie, then fold the cookie in half. Gently press the edges down over the edge of the griddle to create a crease in half. Slide the cookie into a muffin pan cup to hold its shape until cool.
Do not use self-rising flour in this recipe.
Note: These cookies require a flat surface for baking due to their thin batter. If using a slanted or warped griddle, brush a little oil on your pancake turner before turning the first cookie. For crisp fortune cookies, make them on a dry day, ensuring the griddle is hot enough, and grease it with a thin coat of shortening. Make sure to spread the batter very thin.
